description.current: Aggregate team validation metrics: posture scores, coverage %, regressions, journey ownership. Identifies critical projects (score <60). Use for CI/CD reporting, team reviews, regression tracking.
issues: Under-specified: no indication of *when* to trigger dashboard vs when metrics are stale
Missing mention of team-metrics.sh dependency—assumes users know scripts exist in /scripts/
No guardrails on missing .vf/team/projects.json—error behavior undefined

issues.description: Architecture diagram references non-existent scripts: collect-team-metrics.sh, team-dashboard.sh, assign-ownership.sh are listed but live in /scripts/, not bundled here. Diagram misleads user about file structure.

Data availability note admits benchmark format is incomplete—coverage_pct, regression_count display as 0 in real use. This is a major limitation that should be prominent in triggers, not buried in a note.
Workflow section assumes .vf/team/projects.json exists; if it doesn't, script creates template but skill gives no guidance on adding projects before first run.
Step 3 uses informal urgency language ('Critical', 'Needs attention') but never defines score thresholds precisely—are they hardcoded in the scripts or configurable? Ambiguous.
The skill references posture_score but never explains how it's weighted (coverage, evidence quality, enforcement, speed per README). Links to /validate-benchmark but doesn't explain the relationship.
Quick reference table lists 'Shared team registry' option but doesn't explain git/shared storage setup—users may struggle with CI/CD integration.

strengths: Clear step-by-step workflow with actionable commands
Data format specifications (snapshot.json, ownership.json) are precise and machine-readable
Ownership assignment feature is concrete and useful for team accountability
Color-coded urgency levels (Critical < 60, Needs Attention 60–79) are intuitive

bundled_resources.missing_scripts_opportunity: collect-team-metrics.sh, team-dashboard.sh, assign-ownership.sh should be either bundled here or cross-referenced with live path validation

top_fixes: Move data availability note to frontmatter description or early warning—users must know coverage_pct is 0 before running
Add explicit file structure diagram showing where /scripts/team-dashboard.sh actually lives (relative to skill directory)
Define posture_score calculation inline or link directly to /validate-benchmark source, not just the README
Add error handling guidance: what if projects.json is missing? What if a project path is invalid?

test_prompts.prompt: We have 5 projects across web, iOS, and backend. I need a dashboard showing validation posture so the team lead can spot which ones need urgent attention before next sprint planning. Set it up.
test_prompts.expected_output: User runs collect-team-metrics.sh, edits .vf/team/projects.json to add all 5 projects with paths, runs team-dashboard.sh, gets color-coded table with Critical/Attention/Healthy buckets. Dashboard shows scores, regressions, coverage (though coverage will be 0 until /validate-benchmark outputs enriched format).

test_prompts.prompt: Our validation scores show project-alpha at 42%, but I don't know who's supposed to be fixing the failing journeys. Assign ownership so accountability is clear.
test_prompts.expected_output: User runs assign-ownership.sh with --project project-alpha --journey login-flow --owner alice for each failing journey. assign-ownership.sh appends to .vf/team/ownership.json. Next team-dashboard.sh run shows ownership column in table.

test_prompts.prompt: I've modified validation rules across all projects—I need to re-benchmark everything and see which projects regressed. What's the quickest path?
test_prompts.expected_output: User runs team-dashboard.sh --no-collect to skip metrics collection if snapshot is fresh, or re-runs collect-team-metrics.sh first to refresh all project snapshots. Table shows new regression_count column. (Note: regression detection depends on enriched benchmark format—may be 0 until that's implemented.)
